page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a base frequency of 3.1 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth for large-block read-only operations is also strong at 209.21 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core metrics, including Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core performance, fall within the average range. Large-model LLM inference data is unavailable, while text generation for small 135M models registers in the bottom 25% tier at 56.49 tokens per second.

From a resource allocation perspective, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio delivers high compute density, making it a cost-efficient option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the complete absence of local storage represents a key tradeoff, requiring users to factor in the cost and latency of external block storage. The instance is suited for multi-threaded batch processing, database operations, and parallelized compilation tasks, but is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or local-storage-dependent applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 3.1 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the KVM hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but provides a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark evaluations show strong multi-core CPU performance and large-block read memory bandwidth, both ranking in the top 25% tier, while single-core performance and web serving throughput remain average. The 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio makes this instance cost-efficient for high-density compute tasks, though the lack of local storage requires external block storage. It is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, showing strong multi-core performance in benchmarks.

tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a balanced two gigabytes of memory per core.

128.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
